Core library#

Types#

Type aliases for array types used throughout the QFab pipeline.

QHOT.lib.types.Field#

alias of ndarray

QHOT.lib.types.Hologram#

alias of ndarray[tuple[int, …], dtype[uint8]]

QHOT.lib.types.Shape#

alias of tuple[int, int]

QHOT.lib.types.Position#

alias of ndarray[tuple[int, …], dtype[float64]]

QHOTScreen#

class QHOT.lib.QHOTScreen.QHOTScreen(*args, **kwargs)[source]#

Bases: QVideoScreen

Video screen with overlay for interacting with optical traps.

Extends QVideoScreen by adding a QTrapOverlay that intercepts mouse and wheel events and translates them into trap operations.

Parameters:

  • *args – Positional arguments forwarded to QVideoScreen.

  • **kwargs – Keyword arguments forwarded to QVideoScreen.

overlay#

Graphical overlay for creating, moving, and grouping traps.

Type:

QTrapOverlay

Signals#
-------
status#

Emitted with a human-readable message after user actions such as clearing all traps.

Type:

str

rendered#

Emitted each time a video frame is actually drawn, at the rate set by framerate. Suitable for driving computations that should be synchronized with the display cadence.

Type:

signal

status#

Emitted with a status message string for display in the UI.

rendered#

Emitted after each video frame is rendered to the screen.

setImage(image)[source]#

Render a frame and emit rendered if the display rate allows it.

Return type:

None

clearTraps()[source]#

Remove all traps from the overlay and emit a status message.

Return type:

None

mousePressEvent(event)[source]#
Return type:

None

mouseMoveEvent(event)[source]#
Return type:

None

mouseReleaseEvent(event)[source]#
Return type:

None

wheelEvent(event)[source]#
Return type:

None

QSLM#

class QHOT.lib.QSLM.QSLM(*args, fake=False, **kwargs)[source]#

Bases: GraphicsLayoutWidget

Spatial Light Modulator interface

QSLM displays 8-bit phase patterns on an SLM that is configured as the secondary display of the computer. If no secondary display is detected, a window is opened on the primary screen.

shape#

The shape of the SLM in pixels (height, width).

Type:

tuple[int, int]

data#

The current phase pattern displayed on the SLM.

Type:

npt.NDArray[np.uint8]

setData(hologram: npt.NDArray[np.uint8]) → None[source]#

Sets the phase pattern to be displayed on the SLM.

property shape: tuple[int, int]#

Current dimensions of the SLM window.

Returns:

Shape – (height, width) in pixels.

setData(hologram)[source]#

Display a phase hologram on the SLM.

Parameters:

hologram (npt.NDArray[np.uint8]) – Phase pattern to display, encoded as 8-bit integers.

Raises:

ValueError – If hologram.shape does not match the SLM dimensions.

Return type:

None

property data: ndarray[tuple[int, ...], dtype[uint8]]#

Return the phase pattern currently displayed on the SLM.

Returns:

npt.NDArray[np.uint8] – The current image data from the underlying ImageItem.

classmethod example()[source]#

Display a test pattern on the SLM.

Opens an SLM window in fake (primary screen) mode and displays a diagonal gradient pattern. Can be called on any subclass.

Return type:

None

QSLMWidget#

class QHOT.lib.QSLMWidget.QSLMWidget(*args, **kwargs)[source]#

Bases: GraphicsLayoutWidget

Preview widget showing the hologram currently displayed on the SLM.

Embeds a read-only image view of the phase pattern being sent to the physical SLM. Connect QSLM.hologramReady (or any signal carrying a Hologram array) to setData() to keep the preview in sync.

The widget skips updates while hidden so that it imposes no rendering cost when it is not visible.

Inherits#

pyqtgraph.GraphicsLayoutWidget

Parameters:

*args, **kwargs – Forwarded to GraphicsLayoutWidget.

data#

The phase pattern currently shown, or None before the first call to setData().

Type:

Hologram or None

property data: ndarray[tuple[int, ...], dtype[uint8]] | None#

Phase pattern currently shown, or None before first update.

showEvent(event)[source]#
Return type:

None

setData(hologram)[source]#

Display a phase hologram in the preview.

Always caches the hologram so the display is current when the widget becomes visible. Rendering is skipped while hidden.

Parameters:

hologram (Hologram) – Phase pattern to preview, encoded as 8-bit integers.

Return type:

None

classmethod example()[source]#

Show the widget with a diagonal gradient test pattern.

Return type:

None

QSaveFile#

class QHOT.lib.QSaveFile.QSaveFile(parent)[source]#

Bases: QObject

Utility for saving images and TOML configuration files.

Automatically creates a timestamped data directory (~/data) and a hidden configuration directory (~/.{parent_classname}) on construction.

Parameters:

parent (QtWidgets.QMainWindow) – The application main window. Its class name is used to derive the configuration directory.

formats: str = 'PNG Image (*.png);;JPEG Image (*.jpg *.jpeg);;TIFF Image (*.tif *.tiff)'#
trap_format: str = 'Trap Configuration (*.json)'#
static timestamp()[source]#

Return the current date and time as a compact string.

Return type:

str

filename(prefix=None, suffix='')[source]#

Build a timestamped filename in the data directory.

Parameters:

  • prefix (str or None) – Leading name component. Defaults to the parent class name.

  • suffix (str) – File extension including the leading dot (e.g. '.png'). Default: ''.

Return type:

str

Returns:

str – Absolute path string of the form ~/data/{prefix}_{timestamp}{suffix}.

configname(qobj)[source]#

Return the TOML configuration file path for a QObject.

Parameters:

qobj (QtCore.QObject) – Object whose class name determines the filename.

Return type:

str

Returns:

str – Absolute path string of the form ~/.{classname}/{QObjectClass}.toml.

image(data, filename=None, prefix='pyfab')[source]#

Save image data to a file.

Accepts either a pyqtgraph ImageItem (exported via ImageExporter) or a raw numpy array (saved via pg.makeQImage).

Parameters:

  • data (ImageItem or numpy.ndarray) – Image to save.

  • filename (str or None) – Destination path. If None, a timestamped .png file is created in the data directory.

  • prefix (str) – Prefix for the auto-generated filename. Default: 'pyfab'.

Return type:

str

Returns:

str – Path of the file that was written.

imageAs(data, prefix='pyfab')[source]#

Save image data to a user-chosen file via a Save As dialog.

Parameters:

  • data (ImageItem or numpy.ndarray) – Image to save.

  • prefix (str) – Prefix for the default filename suggestion. Default: 'pyfab'.

Return type:

str

Returns:

str – Path of the file that was written, or empty string if cancelled.

traps(overlay, filename=None)[source]#

Save a trap overlay to a JSON file.

Parameters:

  • overlay (QTrapOverlay) – Overlay whose traps will be saved.

  • filename (str or None) – Destination path. If None, a timestamped .json file is created in the data directory.

Return type:

str

Returns:

str – Path of the file that was written.

trapsAs(overlay)[source]#

Save a trap overlay to a user-chosen JSON file via a Save As dialog.

Parameters:

overlay (QTrapOverlay) – Overlay whose traps will be saved.

Return type:

str

Returns:

str – Path of the file that was written, or empty string if cancelled.

openTraps(overlay)[source]#

Load traps from a user-chosen JSON file via an Open dialog.

Parameters:

overlay (QTrapOverlay) – Overlay to populate with the loaded traps.

Return type:

str

Returns:

str – Path of the file that was read, or empty string if cancelled.

toToml(qobj)[source]#

Save the settings of a QObject to a TOML configuration file.

Parameters:

qobj (QtCore.QObject) – Object whose settings property will be serialized.

Return type:

str

Returns:

str – Path of the configuration file that was written.

fromToml(qobj)[source]#

Restore the settings of a QObject from a TOML configuration file.

Parameters:

qobj (QtCore.QObject) – Object whose settings property will be populated.

Return type:

str

Returns:

str – Path of the configuration file that was read, or empty string if the file does not exist.
